name: Borosilicate Glass Laser Cleaning Dataset
description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for Borosilicate Glass. Includes thermal, optical, mechanical, and laser interaction properties validated against industry standards.

license.name: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International
license.description: Free to share and adapt with attribution

measurementTechnique: Laser ablation testing, material characterization, spectroscopy

includedInDataCatalog.name: Z-Beam Material Properties Database
includedInDataCatalog.description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for industrial applications

powerRange.description: Optimal average power for Borosilicate Glass surface cleaning without thermal damage

wavelength.description: Near-IR wavelength providing good transmission control for surface contamination removal

spotSize.description: Beam spot diameter for precise cleaning control on glass surface

repetitionRate.description: Optimal repetition rate for efficient cleaning with thermal management

fluenceThreshold.description: Fluence threshold for contaminant removal without glass damage

pulseWidth.description: Nanosecond pulse duration for efficient contaminant removal with minimal substrate heating

scanSpeed.description: Optimal scanning speed for uniform cleaning coverage

passCount.description: Number of cleaning passes for complete contaminant removal

overlapRatio.description: Optimal beam overlap for uniform cleaning without excessive energy deposition

power.description: Typical range for industrial cleaning of delicate substrates like borosilicate glass to prevent thermal cracking; starting at 50W for light contamination, max safe below 200W to avoid substrate heating beyond 500°C (IPG Photonics laser cleaning systems specs; borosilicate's low thermal conductivity ~1 W/mK limits heat dissipation).

scan_speed.description: Effective for contaminant removal without excessive dwell time; slower (500-1000 mm/s) for heavy contamination, faster (up to 5000 mm/s) for light; recommended 2000 mm/s balances efficiency and safety (industry standards from Laserax and Adapt Laser manuals; considers glass reflectivity ~4% at 1064 nm).

pulse_width.description: Nanosecond regime preferred over ps/fs for cost-effective cleaning; 10-50 ns minimizes heat-affected zone (HAZ <10 μm) while ablating contaminants; avoids fs for industrial scalability (Laser Physics research, e.g., studies on ns pulses for glass in Opt. Express 2018; borosilicate's melting point ~820°C requires short pulses to limit conduction).

repetition_rate.description: Typical for pulsed cleaning; 20-50 kHz recommended to reduce heat accumulation in low-conductivity glass; higher rates (>50 kHz) speed up process but risk cumulative heating (trade-off noted in academic literature, e.g., J. Laser Appl. 2020; balances with glass's thermal diffusivity ~0.6 mm²/s).

energy_density.description: Fluence range for safe contaminant removal; damage threshold ~8-12 J/cm² for borosilicate at 1064 nm ns pulses; recommended 1-3 J/cm² avoids ablation/cracking (ablation threshold studies in Appl. Surf. Sci. 2015; low absorption at 1064 nm (~10 cm⁻¹) means fluence targets surface contaminants primarily).

spot_size.description: Beam diameter for precise surface preparation; smaller (100-200 μm) for fine details, larger (up to 500 μm) for broad areas; 200 μm recommended for uniform cleaning on glass (equipment specifications from Coherent and Trumpf lasers; suits borosilicate's smooth surface to minimize edge effects).

pass_count.description: Single pass for light contamination, multi-pass (2-3) for heavy; recommended 2 passes ensures complete removal without damage (industry case studies on glass cleaning, e.g., proceedings from ICALEO 2019; accounts for glass's high fracture toughness but sensitivity to repeated heating).

overlap_ratio.description: Ensures uniform coverage and avoids streaks; 50-70% for light cleaning, up to 80% for heavy; 70% optimal for borosilicate to prevent uneven heating (process optimization studies in J. Clean. Prod. 2021; standard in scanning systems to maintain consistent fluence distribution).
